headline: Micron, Anthropic sign AI infrastructure supply agreement
summary: Micron Technology said on Monday it has signed an agreement with Anthropic that includes supply ‌of memory and storage products, and a strategic investment ‌in the IPO-bound AI company's latest funding round.  AI developers are racing to secure ​critical components for increasingly expensive data-center buildouts, while memory makers look to tap into the soaring demand for high-bandwidth memory and storage used in training and running advanced AI models.  "Our compute strategy depends ‌on getting every layer ⁠of the stack right, and memory and storage are central to how efficiently we can train ⁠and serve Claude," said Tom Brown, Anthropic's co-founder and chief compute officer.

"Our compute strategy depends ‌on getting every layer ⁠of the stack right, and memory and storage are central to how efficiently we can train ⁠and serve Claude," said Tom Brown, Anthropic's co-founder and chief compute officer.

headline: SK Hynix overtakes Samsung to become South Korea's most valuable company
summary: SK Hynix on Monday overtook Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company, marking a dramatic reversal of fortunes for a chipmaker that two decades ago nearly collapsed under debt.  The company, now the dominant supplier of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips used in AI systems for customers such as ‌Nvidia and Alphabet's Google, has emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries of the global AI boom, propelling a more than 340% rally in its shares this year and lifting ‌its market value above both Samsung Electronics and Micron.  Shares of SK Hynix, now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker, traded up 5.7% to bring the company's market capitalisation to 2,082.5 trillion won ($1.35 trillion) as of 0347 GMT, compared with gains of ​0.4% in Samsung Electronics to 2,081.3 trillion won, excluding preferred shares.

raw.headline: SK Hynix overtakes Samsung to become South Korea's most valuable company

Shares of SK Hynix, now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker, traded up 5.7% to bring the company's market capitalisation to 2,082.5 trillion won ($1.35 trillion) as of 0347 GMT, compared with gains of ​0.4% in Samsung Electronics to 2,081.3 trillion won, excluding preferred shares.

headline: SK Hynix dethrones Samsung Electronics as South Korea's most valuable company
summary: The market value of South Korean chipmaker SK ‌Hynix briefly topped that of peer ‌Samsung Electronics on Monday to make it the ​country's most valuable firm. Shares of SK Hynix, now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker, traded up 5.7% to bring the company's ‌market capitalisation ⁠to 2,082.5 trillion won ($1.35 trillion) as of 0347 GMT, compared with ⁠gains of 0.4% in Samsung Electronics to 2081.3 trillion won, excluding preferred shares. Samsung ​Electronics had ​held the top ​spot since 2000.

Samsung ​Electronics had ​held the top ​spot since 2000.
